cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out the answer to this question about the International Olympic Committee (IOC) being concerned about something regarding the 2008 Olympic medals. Let me read through the provided information again carefully. The question is asking what the IOC were concerned about when the jade ring design was proposed. The options are A to D. From the text: "Experts from the International Olympic Committee (IOC) were concerned about the risk of fragility when the jade ring design was proposed. The IOC required that jade rings should be strong enough to withstand a 2-meter drop test." So the main point here is that the IOC was worried about the fragility of the jade rings. Fragility means something is easily broken or damaged. The text also mentions that the Shanghai Mint even increased the test requirement from 2 meters to 3 meters. Looking at the options: A) the medals will be worth less – The passage doesn't mention anything about the value of the medals. It's about the construction and durability, not monetary value. So probably not A. B) the medals are too difficult to make – While the text does say that bonding jade and metal is not easy, the IOC's concern specifically was about fragility, not the difficulty of manufacturing. So B might not be the correct answer. C) competitors don't like the design – There's no mention of competitors' opinions in the text. The concern came from the IOC experts, not the athletes. So C is likely incorrect. D) jade is fragile and easy to break up – This directly relates to the "risk of fragility" mentioned
